examples.title: Basic Pointers and References
examples.description: Demonstrates basic declaration and usage of pointers and references in C++
examples.explanation:
1. We declare an integer variable x with value 10
2. A pointer ptr is declared using int* and initialized with &x (address of x)
3. A reference ref is declared using int& and binds to x
4. The & operator gives the memory address, * dereferences to get the value
5. Modifying *ptr or ref also modifies the original variable x

examples.title: Pass by Value, Pointer, and Reference
examples.description: Demonstrates the difference between pass by value, pointer, and reference in function parameters
examples.explanation:
1. passByValue: Creates a copy, original variable unchanged (a remains 10)
2. passByPointer: Receives address with &, dereferences to modify original (b becomes 200)
3. passByReference: Creates an alias, directly modifies original (c becomes 300)
4. Pointers require & when passing and * when accessing
5. References are cleaner syntax, behave like the original variable

examples.title: Dynamic Memory Allocation with Pointers
examples.description: Demonstrates dynamic memory allocation, array handling with pointers, and pointer arithmetic
examples.explanation:
1. new int(50) allocates heap memory for a single integer
2. new int[size] creates a dynamic array
3. Array name decays to pointer to first element
4. Pointer arithmetic: *(arr + i) is equivalent to arr[i]
5. int** creates pointer to array of pointers (2D dynamic array)
6. const int* - value cannot be changed through this pointer
7. int* const - pointer address cannot be changed
8. Always use delete to free heap memory to prevent leaks
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for array traversal, O(1) for single operations
